# BTC Consulting — llms.txt

> Jedyna polska firma z własną technologią HTC (Hydrothermal Carbonization) dla oczyszczalni ścieków.

## Company

BTC Consulting sp. z o.o. is a Polish cleantech company specializing in Hydrothermal Carbonization (HTC) technology for municipal wastewater treatment plants (WWTP). Based in Gliwice, Poland, the company holds exclusive rights to HTC technology developed over 10+ years of research with AGH University of Science and Technology in Kraków.

## Core Technology: HTC (Hydrothermal Carbonization)

HTC converts sewage sludge into hydrochar (hydrowęgiel) through a pressure-thermal process:
- **Parameters:** 200°C / 2.0 MPa / 8–12 hours
- **Volume reduction:** 75% (4× less mass)
- **Output:** Hydrochar — a stable, safe, carbon-rich material usable as fertilizer, alternative fuel (SRF/RDF), or construction aggregate
- **Legal status in Poland:** Legal since 14 January 2025 as a fertilizer product
- **First commercial installation:** MPWiK Lubin, 2025 (800 t/year capacity)

## Key Value Proposition

- Cost reduction: PLN 550/t → PLN 200–225/t (−60% disposal costs)
- ROI: 6.9 years with 85% EU grant co-financing
- EU grants cover 75–90% of CapEx (FEnIKS, KPO, NFOŚiGW, LIFE programs)
- Grant window: 2026–2028

## Hub & Spoke Model

For municipalities below 20,000 PE (population equivalent), BTC Consulting offers a regional Hub & Spoke model:
- One central HTC installation serves 3–12 surrounding municipalities within 50 km
- Cost savings: 36–41% compared to individual installations
- Shared EU grant applications for the entire group

## Services

1. **TEO (Techno-Economic Assessment):** 4–6 weeks, 200+ page documentation ready for NFOŚiGW submission
2. **HTC Installation Design & Delivery:** Turnkey HTC systems in three configurations (HTC-S, HTC-D, TH+HTC)
3. **Grant Application Support:** Documentation for FEnIKS, KPO, NFOŚiGW, LIFE programs
4. **ROI Calculator:** Free online tool at btcconsulting.pl/v5/kalkulator-roi.html

## Market Context

- UWWTD 2024 (Urban Waste Water Treatment Directive) requires 3,873 Polish WWTPs to modernize by 2035
- Poland must comply with new EU sludge management regulations
- Current sludge disposal costs are rising (PLN 550/t average)
